key_insight: LLMs can help build a working MaxSAT solver directly from research papers, but the process still needs human guidance and validation.
engineering_relevance: The work shows a practical path for using LLMs to accelerate solver prototyping and code generation for combinatorial optimization systems.
method: Iterative paper discussion with ChatGPT to derive the algorithm design
Codex-driven implementation of solver components and MaxSAT routines
Repeated LLM-assisted audits and revisions, including core-guided optimization, preprocessing, core minimization, ILP backend integration, and lookahead
evidence_signal: Fuzzing and MaxSAT Evaluation instances did not reveal wrong answers in the tested configurations, though performance lagged behind top hand-engineered solvers.
watch_item: The main concern is that correctness and competitiveness are not guaranteed by LLM-generated code, so external benchmarking and expert review remain necessary.
paper_title: Can LLMs Build a MaxSAT Solver from Papers? The CoreForge Experience
